page: Amazon Web Services m9g.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this server features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The CPU includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong memory and compression capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression (36.5 GB/sec) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (17.09 GB/sec), alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score of 3745.62. However, raw computational benchmarks show some limitations; the PassMark CPU score is weak at 5631.84, and the CPU encryption test is also weak at 1191.63. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, stress-ng CPU tests, and Redis SET operations (12.65 million operations per second) fall within the average range. For machine learning, LLM inference on a small 135M model is average, but prompt processing on a medium 7B model drops to a poor tier at 11.27 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m9g.xlarge leverages the ARMv9 architecture to deliver high memory bandwidth and compression efficiency relative to its resource footprint, though it lacks local storage and high-speed network interfaces. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, database operations, and lightweight data compression tasks. It is less suited for heavy cryptographic operations or medium-to-large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16.0 GB of system memory, 2 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and memory bandwidth, alongside average performance in database operations and static web serving. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in PassMark CPU and encryption tests, and struggles with medium-sized LLM prompt processing. This server is suited for general-purpose application hosting, web serving, and memory-bound tasks that benefit from high memory bandwidth and ARM efficiency.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton ARMv9 cores, 16 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory and compression performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 4 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 16 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated ARMv9 cores and high memory bandwidth for efficient web serving and data compression.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 48 MB of L3 cache
Top-tier performance in single-threaded compression and memory bandwidth tests
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage or GPU
Suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and database operations
